plan_template.goal: Inspect the source issue and summarize the requested change.
plan_template.acceptance_criteria: The issue requirements and constraints are captured for the wave.

plan_template.goal: Review the proposed design for correctness before implementation.
plan_template.acceptance_criteria: Channel a records a design verdict.

plan_template.goal: Review the proposed design for failure paths before implementation.
plan_template.acceptance_criteria: Channel b records a design verdict.

plan_template.goal: Create a worktree, implement the change, and commit the result.
plan_template.acceptance_criteria: The change is committed in the wave worktree.

plan_template.goal: Open a pull request and check its diff/check status.
plan_template.acceptance_criteria: A pull request exists with readable diff and check status.

plan_template.goal: Review the pull request for correctness.
plan_template.acceptance_criteria: Channel a records a PR verdict.

plan_template.goal: Review the pull request for failure paths.
plan_template.acceptance_criteria: Channel b records a PR verdict.

plan_template.goal: Merge the approved pull request and close the issue.
plan_template.acceptance_criteria: The approved PR is merged and the issue is closed.

workflows.spec_instructions:
For wave {wave_id}, drive dual-review convergence for each review subject.

After BOTH channels for a phase complete, call calm.review.round with subject:{phase,slice_id,pr_number?}, optional head_sha, n, cap, converged, channels:[both verdicts], and root_cause when known. Record each channel's verdict as the literal lowercase token `approved` or `changes_requested` (exactly those strings). converged is true only when EVERY channel verdict is `approved`. For PR subjects, head_sha is the reviewed forge.pr.diff.read head_sha; omit head_sha for design subjects.

For each subject, set n to the last observed review.round n for that same subject plus 1. cap is the fixed policy constant 8.

Always re-review. Every fix re-dispatches BOTH channels before the next calm.review.round.

Merge fence F4: call gh.pr.merge for a subject ONLY when that subject's latest review.round has converged:true. Pass expected_head_sha equal to that round's head_sha.

If n == cap and the round is non-approving, do not merge. Either GIVE-UP with calm.plan.upsert lifecycle failed for reviewing->failed, terminal; OR ASK-HUMAN by first moving reviewing->working with the normal lifecycle arg, then call calm.ratify.request with reason:"cap_exhausted" for working->blocked. On ratify.resolved grant, resume blocked->working->reviewing and continue until the subject may converge and merge.

Record root_cause each round; repeated facets should drive a class fix.
